anti_templating_check: Two pages read as a contrasting pair within the entity-exit decision cluster. A19 SSE is a DEEPER STATUTORY-MECHANICS REFERENCE LAYER for advisor/FD audience, single statutory regime (Sch 7AC family), 5 worked examples all SSE-specific (multi-DevCo HoldCo FA2017 broadening; QII para 7A institutional-fund; s.179 FA2011 modification; loss-symmetry; asset-strip para 5), 12 FAQs, anonymised personas Threadwell + Marshfield Capital + Eastfield + Pilcrow + Cranbridge, 8-section structure. A20 transferring-business-out is a HONEST 5-ROUTE DECISION FRAME for founder/FD/advisor audience, comparative across 5 statutory regimes, 5 worked examples all 5-route-comparison (Frampton £500k 5-routes; Dunlevy strike-off hybrid; Burnham phoenix TAAR; Hatherfield bona vacantia; Vorlund VAT CGS), 14 FAQs reflecting decision-frame breadth, anonymised personas Frampton + Dunlevy + Burnham + Hatherfield + Vorlund (NO overlap with A19 personas), 11-section structure. Cross-link directionality: A20 forward-links to A19 for SSE-route depth + s.179 deeper mechanics; A19 cross-links to A20 for broader entity-exit decision frame. Pillar→specialist relationship consistent. No template-feel; each page holds its distinctive operational angle. Honest framing discipline held across both — A19 acknowledges QII carve-out + HIGH cannibal risk with existing page openly; A20 leads with disincorporation-relief-is-dead + surfaces triple-charge trap + bona vacantia + phoenix TAAR cite-correctness + VAT CGS clawback as the load-bearing realities rather than soft-pedalling.

discoveries_logged: D-21 SEVENTH CONSECUTIVE STAGE-2-THEN-RUN BATCH §16.36 + §16.40 DISCIPLINE HELD — F-11 §16.40 D-13 phoenix-TAAR ITTOIA-2005-s.396B cite-correction has now propagated CLEANLY through TWO consecutive batches (M2-A-B3 → M2-A-B4) from Stage 2 brief authoring → RUN page authoring. Pattern observation: where Stage 2 catches AND propagates a cite-correction, RUN author internalisation is high; the corrected cite enters the load-bearing reality of the RUN draft + the do-not-write GREP discipline list AND the body prose AND defensive cite-correctness callouts. Future Stage 1 dispatch prompts could enumerate known-drift watchpoints as a worked-example pre-emptive correction list (extension of D-17 recommendation) — specific candidate watchpoints for next-batch dispatch: (i) ITTOIA 2005 s.396B not ITA 2007 s.396B [F-11]; (ii) HMRC PM276000 = CIS not salaried-member [F-9]; (iii) HMRC PM210000+ + PM213000+ for mixed-membership [F-10]; (iv) HMRC PM250000+ for salaried-member [F-9]; (v) PM236500 does not exist [F-9]; (vi) ss.464C/D omitted from s.455 mechanic if cited [F-7]; (vii) dividend rates 10.75/35.75/39.35 not 8.75/33.75/39.35 [F-20]; (viii) secondary Class 1 NIC 15% not 13.8% [F-19]; (ix) s.131 route via s.152 not direct cite [Wave 7]; (x) disincorporation relief s.162B EXPIRED 31 March 2018 not still available [F-A-B4 RUN observation]. This watchpoint list is the operational embedding of the §16.40 pattern discipline.

D-22 BUCKET A SESSION-A FULL-RUN COMPLETE — All 20 picks in Bucket A (M2-A-B1 through M2-A-B4) now have FINAL PUBLISHED BLOG PAGES committed to property-megawave2-a worktree branch. Complete entity-choice + entity-operation + entity-exit decision frame is now in place at the published-page layer (not just at the Stage 2 brief layer per D-18). Cluster maturity verified at the published-page layer: A18 (sole-trader-vs-partnership early fork) → A7 (partnership-existence definitional) → A16 (partnership-agreement architecture) → A14/A15 (LLP-specific) → A1 (LtdCo formation pillar) → A12 (LtdCo entity pillar) → A4/A5/A6 (LtdCo planning + marginal relief + DLA mechanics) → A11 (HMO-specific LtdCo overlay) → A13 (LtdCo BTL operational handbook) → A17 (CT registration entry-point) → A19 (SSE specialist exit-route depth) → A20 (entity-exit decision frame mirror to A1). The MIRROR-relationship between A1 (INTO-LtdCo formation pillar) and A20 (OUT-OF-LtdCo entity-exit decision frame) is now operationally complete at the published-page layer with reciprocal cross-link discipline.

D-23 PHOENIX TAAR CITE-CORRECTNESS DEFENSIVE PROSE PATTERN EMERGENT — A20 RUN authoring uses 3 intentional defensive callouts of the wrong cite ('ITA 2007 s.396B does not exist') alongside 15 correct usages of ITTOIA 2005 s.396B. Pattern observation: where a cite-correction is load-bearing and the wrong cite is in common circulation in older summaries, defensive prose that explicitly calls out the wrong cite (and explains why it is wrong) is a cleaner authoring discipline than simply using the correct cite silently. The defensive-callout pattern teaches readers + future RUN authors that the cite-correction matters; the silent-correct-usage pattern relies on readers internalising the correction without context. For future MW2 RUN authoring, when a watchpoint cite has a circulating wrong variant, the defensive-callout discipline could be adopted as a standard pattern. Specific candidates for defensive callouts: ITTOIA 2005 s.396B (this batch), HMRC PM276000 = CIS not salaried-member, dividend rates 10.75/35.75/39.35 not 8.75/33.75/39.35, secondary Class 1 NIC 15% not 13.8%.

D-24 §21.X UNIFIED ENTITY-EXIT LOCK CANDIDATE — INHERITED FROM D-20 — A20 RUN authoring confirms the operational utility of a §21.X unified entity-exit lock candidate: A20 consumes 12+ existing HP locks (§1 + §1.I + §11 + §11.A + §21 + §21.A + §21.1 + §21.4 + §21.5 + §21.8 + §22 + §22.A + §25 + §25.11 + §27 + §28 + §29) for a single decision frame. The case for §21.X is operational discoverability (single anchor for the entity-exit decision cluster spanning A20 + MVL depth + EOT depth + SSE sibling + portfolio-exit + pre-sale extraction + extraction-sequence pillar + time-pressure extraction). The case against is that the existing locks cleanly map the underlying tax mechanics + a meta-anchor lock would be substantive-less. Inherited defer to manager — propose §21.X be considered at the post-Bucket-A review point when the cumulative entity-exit cluster maturity provides the strongest evidence base for the meta-anchor decision.
